title: Chess
rulesMd: Two players move distinct pieces on an 8x8 board. The goal is to checkmate the opposing king while protecting your own king from capture threats.

title: Checkers
rulesMd: Players move diagonally across the board, capturing opposing pieces by jumping over them. Reaching the far side promotes a piece and the game ends when one player cannot move.

title: Go
rulesMd: Players alternate placing stones on intersections, surrounding territory and capturing groups with no liberties. The winner controls more territory after both players pass.

title: Backgammon
rulesMd: Players roll dice to move checkers around the board and bear them off. Blocking, hitting exposed pieces and racing efficiently decide the winner.

title: Monopoly
rulesMd: Players move around the board buying properties, charging rent and developing sets. The game ends when opponents go bankrupt or by an agreed time/wealth condition.

title: Scrabble
rulesMd: Players place letter tiles in crossword patterns to score words. Premium squares and tile values shape the best plays.

title: Risk
rulesMd: Players deploy armies, attack territories with dice and try to complete objectives or conquer the map. Reinforcement timing and continent control are central.

title: Clue
rulesMd: Players gather clues by moving through rooms and making suggestions. The winner identifies the suspect, weapon and room before the others.

title: The Settlers of Catan
rulesMd: Players build settlements and roads, collect resources from dice rolls and trade to reach victory points through expansion and development.

title: Ticket to Ride
rulesMd: Players collect colored train cards to claim routes and complete destination tickets. Points come from routes, tickets and longest continuous railway.

title: Carcassonne
rulesMd: Players place landscape tiles and meeples to score cities, roads, monasteries and fields. Timing meeple placement is the main tactical choice.

title: Pandemic
rulesMd: Players cooperate as specialists to treat diseases, share knowledge and discover cures before outbreaks or card pressure overwhelm the team.

title: 7 Wonders
rulesMd: Players draft cards over three ages, building resources, science, military, commerce and civic structures to score the most points.

title: Dominion
rulesMd: Players build decks by buying cards from a shared market. Efficient card combinations and timing the shift to victory cards decide the winner.

title: Azul
rulesMd: Players draft colored tiles into pattern lines, then score completed rows on their wall. Overdrafting causes penalties, making timing and color denial important.

title: Splendor
rulesMd: Players collect gem tokens to buy development cards, building discounts and prestige. Nobles and efficient engine growth shape the race to victory.

title: Dixit
rulesMd: A storyteller gives a clue for an illustrated card. Others submit similar cards, and players score by balancing guessability with ambiguity.

title: Codenames
rulesMd: Spymasters give one-word clues to help teammates identify their agents while avoiding opposing agents and the assassin.

title: Love Letter
rulesMd: Players hold one card, draw one card and play one each turn, using character effects to eliminate rivals or finish with the highest value.

title: Uno
rulesMd: Players match color or number to discard cards, using action cards to disrupt opponents. The first player to empty their hand wins the round.

title: Apples to Apples
rulesMd: Players submit noun cards to match a prompt adjective, and the judge chooses the funniest or best fit. The role of judge rotates.

title: Cards Against Humanity
rulesMd: Players anonymously submit response cards to complete a prompt. The round judge chooses the preferred response, usually for humor or shock value.

title: Exploding Kittens
rulesMd: Players draw cards while avoiding exploding kitten cards. Action cards manipulate the deck, skip turns or protect with defuse cards.

title: Munchkin
rulesMd: Players kick down doors, fight monsters, gain treasure and interfere with each other while racing to reach the winning level.

title: King of Tokyo
rulesMd: Monsters roll dice to attack, heal, gain energy or score points. Holding Tokyo is powerful but dangerous.

title: Hive
rulesMd: Players place and move insect tiles without a board, trying to surround the opponent queen while respecting movement rules and hive connectivity.

title: Qwirkle
rulesMd: Players place tiles sharing either color or shape, scoring lines and bonuses for completing six-tile sets.

title: Blokus
rulesMd: Players place pieces so their own pieces touch only at corners. The goal is to fit as many pieces as possible before space closes.

title: Sequence
rulesMd: Players play cards to place chips on matching board spaces, trying to form lines while blocking opponents.

title: Othello
rulesMd: Players place discs to bracket and flip opponent discs. The player with more discs of their color when no moves remain wins.

title: Battleship
rulesMd: Players secretly place ships on grids and call coordinates to locate and sink the opponent fleet.

title: Guess Who?
rulesMd: Players ask yes/no questions to eliminate characters and identify the opponent's secret character first.

title: Jenga
rulesMd: Players remove one block at a time from a tower and place it on top. The player who causes the tower to fall loses.

title: Connect Four
rulesMd: Players drop discs into columns, trying to make four in a row horizontally, vertically or diagonally before the opponent.

title: Mastermind
rulesMd: One player sets a hidden code and the other guesses sequences, receiving feedback on correct colors and positions.

title: Yahtzee
rulesMd: Players roll dice up to three times per turn and score combinations on a score sheet. Category timing is the key decision.

title: Boggle
rulesMd: Players find words in adjacent letters before time runs out. Unique valid words score by length.

title: Pictionary
rulesMd: A player draws clues while teammates guess the word or phrase before time expires.

title: Trivial Pursuit
rulesMd: Players answer trivia questions by category, collecting wedges and racing to complete their token.

title: Taboo
rulesMd: Players describe a target word without using forbidden clue words while teammates guess under time pressure.

title: Balderdash
rulesMd: Players invent plausible definitions or answers, then vote among real and fake options to score.

title: Scattergories
rulesMd: Players list words in categories starting with a chosen letter. Unique valid answers score points.

title: The Game of Life
rulesMd: Players move through life events, careers, family and finances, trying to finish with the most wealth.

title: Sorry!
rulesMd: Players move pawns around the board using cards, bumping opponents back to start while trying to bring all pawns home.

title: Trouble
rulesMd: Players race pieces around a track using die rolls, sending opponents back when landing on occupied spaces.

title: Parcheesi
rulesMd: Players race pawns from start to home, using safe spaces, blockades and captures to slow opponents.

title: Ludo
rulesMd: Players move tokens from start to home according to die rolls, capturing opposing tokens by landing on them.

title: Mancala
rulesMd: Players sow stones around pits, capturing or collecting according to the variant. The winner has more stones at the end.

title: Mille Bornes
rulesMd: Players play distance cards while using hazards and remedies to slow opponents, racing to the target distance.

title: Rummikub
rulesMd: Players create runs and groups with numbered tiles, rearranging the table to empty their rack first.

title: Mahjong
rulesMd: Players draw and discard tiles to complete a legal hand, with scoring depending on the variant and hand patterns.

title: Dominoes
rulesMd: Players place matching tiles end to end, scoring or winning by emptying their hand depending on the variant.

title: Mexican Train
rulesMd: Players build personal and shared domino trains, trying to play all tiles while managing playable open trains.

title: Cribbage
rulesMd: Players score card combinations during play and hand counting, tracking points on a peg board.

title: Bridge
rulesMd: Partners bid to set a contract, then play tricks to fulfill or defeat it. Communication through bidding is central.

title: Hearts
rulesMd: Players avoid taking hearts and the queen of spades unless attempting to take all penalty cards.

title: Spades
rulesMd: Partners bid expected tricks and play trick-taking hands where spades are always trump.

title: Euchre
rulesMd: Teams bid trump and try to win enough tricks with a reduced deck where bowers are the highest trump cards.

title: Canasta
rulesMd: Players form melds of equal-rank cards and canastas, drawing and discarding while managing wild cards and the discard pile.

title: Gin Rummy
rulesMd: Players draw and discard to form sets and runs, knocking when unmatched cards are low enough.

title: Skat
rulesMd: Three players bid for the right to declare a contract, then play tricks with trump and card-point scoring.

title: Tichu
rulesMd: Partners shed card combinations, call scoring declarations and use special cards to manage tempo and points.

title: Diplomacy
rulesMd: Players negotiate secretly, then reveal simultaneous orders for movement and support. Trust and betrayal drive the game.

title: Stratego
rulesMd: Players deploy hidden-ranked pieces and move to capture the opposing flag, using deduction and bluffing.

title: Acquire
rulesMd: Players place hotel tiles, buy stock and profit from mergers. Timing investments around growth and acquisitions decides the winner.

title: Power Grid
rulesMd: Players auction power plants, buy resources, expand networks and power cities efficiently across rounds.

title: Puerto Rico
rulesMd: Players choose roles that everyone follows, producing goods, shipping them and building for victory points.

title: Agricola
rulesMd: Players place workers to grow a farm, feed family members and balance crops, animals, rooms and improvements.

title: Stone Age
rulesMd: Players assign workers, roll dice for resources and build huts/cards to score while feeding their tribe.

title: Lords of Waterdeep
rulesMd: Players place agents to collect adventurers, complete quests and use intrigue cards for advantages.

title: Small World
rulesMd: Players choose fantasy race-power combinations, conquer regions, score coins and put declining races aside when expansion stalls.

title: Tigris & Euphrates
rulesMd: Players place leaders and civilization tiles, resolving internal and external conflicts while scoring balanced color points.

title: El Grande
rulesMd: Players secretly bid for turn order, place caballeros in regions and score majorities across Spain.

title: Terraforming Mars
rulesMd: Players draft and play project cards to raise oxygen, temperature and oceans while building engines and scoring milestones.

title: Wingspan
rulesMd: Players attract birds to habitats, gaining food, eggs, cards and points through engine-building combinations.

title: Gloomhaven
rulesMd: Players cooperate through tactical scenarios using card-driven actions, persistent character growth and campaign choices.

title: Scythe
rulesMd: Players expand factions, produce resources, upgrade actions and score territory, popularity and achievements.

title: Root
rulesMd: Asymmetric factions compete for woodland control, each scoring through different actions and strategic tempo.

title: Viticulture
rulesMd: Players assign workers seasonally to plant vines, harvest grapes, make wine and fill orders.

title: Everdell
rulesMd: Players place workers and play critter/construction cards to build a city across seasons.

title: The Quacks of Quedlinburg
rulesMd: Players draw ingredient chips from bags to brew potions, pushing luck without exploding the pot.

title: Mysterium
rulesMd: A ghost gives surreal vision cards while psychics infer suspects, locations and objects cooperatively.

title: Betrayal at House on the Hill
rulesMd: Players explore a haunted house until a haunt triggers, often turning one player into a traitor with special goals.

title: Arkham Horror
rulesMd: Investigators cooperate to manage monsters, clues and gates while trying to prevent an ancient one from awakening.

title: Twilight Imperium
rulesMd: Galactic factions expand, negotiate, vote, fight and complete objectives in a long-form space strategy game.

title: Twilight Struggle
rulesMd: Two players use event cards and operations to influence regions during the Cold War while managing DEFCON risk.

title: Cosmic Encounter
rulesMd: Alien powers break rules asymmetrically as players negotiate alliances and try to establish colonies.

title: Sheriff of Nottingham
rulesMd: Merchants declare goods, bluff about contraband and negotiate with the sheriff, who may inspect bags.

title: Camel Up
rulesMd: Players bet on camel races where stacked camels move unpredictably from dice rolls.

title: Sushi Go!
rulesMd: Players draft sushi cards over rounds, collecting scoring combinations and passing hands each turn.

title: Hanabi
rulesMd: Players cooperatively build fireworks while holding cards facing away from themselves and giving limited clues.

title: The Resistance
rulesMd: Players debate and vote on mission teams while hidden spies try to sabotage missions without being exposed.

title: Werewolf
rulesMd: Hidden-role players alternate night actions and day debates, trying to eliminate the opposing team through deduction and persuasion.

title: Telestrations
rulesMd: Players alternate drawing and guessing prompts, then reveal the chain of misunderstandings for scoring or laughs.

title: Just One
rulesMd: Players cooperatively give one-word clues, but duplicate clues are erased before the guesser sees them.

title: Concept
rulesMd: Players use icons and markers to communicate words or phrases without speaking direct clues.

title: Decrypto
rulesMd: Teams give clues to transmit number codes to teammates while opponents try to intercept the pattern.

title: The Mind
rulesMd: Players cooperatively play numbered cards in ascending order without communicating exact information.

title: Santorini
rulesMd: Players move builders and construct towers, winning by moving onto the third level while using god powers in many variants.

title: Patchwork
rulesMd: Two players buy and place patch tiles on personal boards, balancing buttons, time and empty spaces.

title: Jaipur
rulesMd: Two players trade and sell goods, timing market exchanges and camel use to earn valuable tokens.

title: Lost Cities
rulesMd: Two players play ascending expedition cards, deciding when an expedition is worth starting and when to discard.

title: Onitama
rulesMd: Players move martial arts pieces using shared movement cards, aiming to capture the master or reach the opponent temple.

title: Kingdomino
rulesMd: Players draft domino-like terrain tiles to build compact kingdoms, scoring connected areas with crowns.

title: Forbidden Island
rulesMd: Players cooperate to collect treasures and escape before the island sinks under rising flood pressure.
